notes: TC260 is China's national technical committee for information security standardization. Publishes AI safety governance frameworks.

data.description: China's national AI safety governance framework, published by TC260. Implements the Global AI Governance Initiative.
data.notes: Framework provides a roadmap for AI safety governance, not binding law. Details implemented through subsequent CAC (Cyberspace Administration of China) notices and the Basic Security Requirements national standard. Two overarching risk categories: endemic generative AI risks (inherent to AI) and application generative AI risks (misuse/abuse). Covers frontier risks including CBRN, missile domain, loss of control, and catastrophic risks. Available in Chinese; unofficial translations exist. v2.0 (September 2025) strengthened loss of control and catastrophic risk attention, added open-source AI governance clauses.

versions_available.note: Strengthened loss of control and catastrophic risks, added open-source governance clauses.
Initial framework. Endemic and application risks, CBRN coverage.

children.description: AI Safety Governance Framework topic area (lowercase kebab-case)

data.note: No per-topic URL. Framework topics are sections within the published document. Refer to the TC260 website for the full framework PDF.
